summary: Matched a large slice of Kirby copied neutral-special logic in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd.c. The PR replaced multiple placeholder functions with matching C for Sheik-needle-style spawning, Mewtwo-copy charge/fire/start/loop/IASA behavior, and fn_801090D4, with small cleanup changes that finished several near-matches. decomp.dev reported 18 new matches and +6208 matched bytes, raising matched code by +0.16%.

key_files.role: Only changed file; implemented and tuned matching C for Kirby copied neutral special routines.

what_changed: Added the missing item include it/items/itseakneedlethrown.h and extern declarations for ftKb_Init_803B7570, ftKb_Init_803B757C, ftKb_Init_803CB770, and ftKb_Init_803CB828.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialNSk_8010603C, spawning stored needles from fp->fv.kb.xB4 at ground/air graphic offsets via it_802AFD8C and it_802AFEA8, then clearing the count.

Implemented fn_80106DB0 for single needle release behavior using ground/air x/y offsets, ftKb_Init_803CB770 randomized y-offsets through a small HSD_Randi wrapper, efSync_Spawn(0x503), and ft_PlaySFX.
Introduced ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs to share repeated death2_cb/take_dmg_cb assignment to ftKb_Init_800EE74C and ftKb_Init_800EE7B8 across Kirby special-N setup paths.

Implemented Mewtwo-copy helper logic: ftKb_SpecialNMt_SetRecoil, ftKb_SpecialNMt_801071FC for firing the held projectile from the right-hand joint, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107410 for charge-threshold SFX playback.
Reworked ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568 and ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107638 so their ground/air start actions directly initialize cmd_vars, callbacks, timers, velocity, and animation state in matching form.

Implemented ftKb_MtSpecialNStart_Anim, ftKb_MtSpecialNLoop_Anim, ftKb_MtSpecialAirNStart_Anim, and ftKb_MtSpecialAirNLoop_Anim, including held-shadow item creation, charge accumulation, full-charge transitions, effects, and SFX-related calls.

Implemented ground and air loop IASA handlers for Mewtwo-copy special-N, including A/B release, cancel input 0x80000000, item cleanup via it_802C573C, effect cleanup via efLib_DestroyAll, and ftKirbyDmgInline calls.
Implemented fn_801090D4 using fp->cmd_vars[0] to spawn or release an item with specialn_pp spawn offsets, SFX calls, and callback setup when the item exists.
Finished smaller near-matches by using GET_FIGHTER consistently, NULL for pointer fields, local ftKb_DatAttrs* da, scoped locals in switch cases, and PAD_STACK/dummy locals where needed.
smart_moves: Factored repeated special-N callback setup into an inline helper, reducing duplication while preserving matching output according to the bot report.
Used small inline helpers only where they helped codegen: ftKb_SpecialNMt_SetRecoil, ftKb_SpecialNMt_CreateHeldShadow, and the explicitly noted permuter-generated perm_randi wrapper around HSD_Randi.
Kept non-obvious decomp artifacts such as PAD_STACK calls, dummy f64 locals, u8 padding arrays, redundant GET_FIGHTER calls, and narrow local scopes to force the compiler shape needed for matches.
Separated ground and air variants where their constants and motion states differ, but reused the held-shadow creation helper where codegen allowed it.
Preserved original-looking control flow in IASA cleanup paths, including repeated gobj != NULL checks and repeated GET_FIGHTER calls, rather than over-simplifying the logic.
Used data-symbol externs for Vec3 constants and SFX/random tables instead of hardcoding equivalent literals, which also corresponded with a reported .sdata2 improvement.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action-state code, exact local variable lifetime, scope, and repeated loads can be required for matching; readable simplification may break codegen.
Inline helpers can be useful matching tools, but only when verified against the compiler output; this PR shows both shared real helpers and a fake permuter helper.
Pointer fields in fighter state should use NULL in matched C when the original codegen expects pointer-style zeroing.
Local dat_attrs aliases can be enough to finish partial matches, as shown by the ftKb_SpecialNIc_80108E14 cleanup from 95.52% to 100%.

Kirby copied-special work often crosses fighter state, held items, spawned projectiles, effects, animation callbacks, and SFX; matching one function may require correct includes and extern declarations for several subsystems.
Do not prematurely rename unknown functions: fn_80106DB0 and fn_801090D4 remained address-style names despite clear behavior in the implementation.
naming_conventions: Kept the ftKb_ module prefix for Kirby routines and copied-special prefixes such as SpecialNSk, SpecialNMt, SpecialNIc, MtSpecialN, and MtSpecialAirN.
Retained address-style names fn_80106DB0 and fn_801090D4 where no reviewed semantic name was provided.
Used descriptive static/inline helper names under the same module prefix, including ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs, ftKb_SpecialNMt_SetRecoil, and ftKb_SpecialNMt_CreateHeldShadow.
Used GET_FIGHTER(gobj) as the canonical fighter accessor in rewritten code, replacing getFighter or direct HSD_GObjGetUserData forms where matching required it.
Used NULL for fighter-state item/effect pointers such as fp->fv.kb.x98, xA0, xB8, and xC0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted PAD_STACK with sizes 4, 8, 0x10, 16, and 24 to match stack layout.
Added dummy locals such as f64 _pad1/_pad2 and u8 _[8] to influence stack/register allocation.
Used redundant assignments like Fighter* fp = fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and ftKb_DatAttrs* da = da = fp->dat_attrs in some matched functions.
Scoped variables inside switch cases and nested blocks to control lifetimes and generated code.
Used a fake inline perm_randi wrapper around HSD_Randi, noted in the diff as permuter-generated and likely fake.
Used ternary assignment for specialn_mt_frames_to_transition timer setup instead of a temporary timer variable.
Kept explicit f32 casts and float constants in charge comparisons, including comparisons against da->specialn_mt_charge_time.
Referenced external data tables and constants such as ftKb_Init_803B7570, ftKb_Init_803B757C, ftKb_Init_803CB770, and ftKb_Init_803CB828 to match data access patterns.
Preserved repeated cleanup sequences in IASA functions rather than factoring them into a helper, likely because the original control flow/codegen required duplication.
review_feedback: No human review comments or review records were captured in the PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 18 new matches in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd and +6208 matched bytes.

Largest bot-reported new matches included ftKb_SpecialNMt_801071FC (+532), ftKb_MtSpecialNLoop_Anim (+512), ftKb_MtSpecialAirNLoop_Anim (+512), ftKb_MtSpecialNLoop_IASA (+436), ftKb_MtSpecialNLoopFull_IASA (+412), and fn_80106DB0 (+404).
The bot also reported one unmatched-item improvement: .sdata2 improved by +43 bytes, from 37.50% to 79.07%.
